intro: Barosma betulina gel, also known as Buchu gel, is a topical remedy traditionally used in South African folk medicine to treat various skin conditions, including acne, eczema, and minor wounds. The gel is derived from the leaves of the Buchu plant, which contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. When applied topically, Barosma betulina gel is believed to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe irritated skin.

recipe: To make Barosma betulina gel for medicinal use, the roots of the Barosma betulina plant are typically harvested and then extracted with a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin to obtain the bioactive compounds. The gel is then filtered and sterilized to ensure its safety for medicinal use.
